calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Mar-19-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BRANDON TANG, TEL. NO. (510) 896-9411 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. ALLEN J. KENT, TEL. NO. 272-2662 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY Adjudication - Motion for summary adjudication as to the account stated cause of action is granted. Plaintiff met its initial burden by submitting evidence proving each element of the cause of action. (Plaintiff's Undisputed Material Facts, 1-18.) Defendant does not raise a triable issue of material fact. Defendant's objections to the Declaration of Richard Kier are overruled. The Declaration is admissible pursuant to the business records exception from the hearsay rule. (See Cal. Ev. Code sec. 1271.) The Declaration lays adequate foundation and establishes that Mr. Kier has personal knowledge. Plaintiff has standing to maintain this cause of action. (See Cal. Civ. Code sec. 171; 12 U.S.C. sec. 24.) This cause of action is not barred by the statute of limitations under either California or Utah law. (See CCP sec. 337; Utah Code sec. 76B-2-307.) Utah Code sec. 70C-7-205 is inapplicable to this action. The one year statute of limitations provided for in that section applies to actions brought by debtors against credit card companies. Order and Judgment signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185 =302/ehg

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-20-2015 Continued To Mar-19-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF AMERICAN EXPRESS BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION is continued to March 19, 2015 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than February 20, 2015 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the opposition papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)
